ph.label: pH
ph.note: WHO operational range and EPA secondary standard are 6.5-8.5. No health-based guideline value; extreme pH affects disinfection and is corrosive.

chlorine_residual_ppm.label: Chlorine
chlorine_residual_ppm.note: WHO: minimum 0.2 mg/L free chlorine at point of delivery, at least 0.5 mg/L for effective disinfection, health-based guideline 5 mg/L. EPA MRDL 4 mg/L. Low residual can indicate inadequate disinfection.

turbidity_ntu.label: Turbidity
turbidity_ntu.note: WHO: below 1 NTU is ideal for effective disinfection, below 5 NTU is acceptable. EPA primary standard is 1 NTU. High turbidity shields microorganisms and reduces disinfection effectiveness.

nitrate_ppm.label: Nitrate
nitrate_ppm.note: WHO guideline value 50 mg/L as nitrate ion. EPA MCL 10 mg/L as nitrogen (about 45 mg/L as nitrate). Acute risk of methaemoglobinaemia in bottle-fed infants.

nitrite_ppm.label: Nitrite
nitrite_ppm.note: WHO acute guideline value 3 mg/L as nitrite ion. EPA MCL 1 mg/L as nitrogen.

hardness_ppm.label: Hardness
hardness_ppm.note: No health-based guideline. USGS classes above 180 mg/L as very hard; WHO notes scaling above 200 mg/L as calcium carbonate. Aesthetic and operational only, not a safety risk.

iron_ppm.label: Iron
iron_ppm.note: No health-based guideline. EPA secondary standard 0.3 mg/L; WHO aesthetic threshold around 0.3 mg/L for staining and taste.
